target.description: A target is a rule that defines what to build, its dependencies, and how to build it.

phony.description: Declares targets that don't represent files. Prevents conflicts with files of the same name.

variable_simple.description: Simple expansion variable. Value is expanded at the time of assignment.

variable_recursive.description: Recursive expansion variable. Value is expanded each time the variable is referenced.

variable_conditional.description: Conditional assignment. Only sets the variable if it is not already defined.

variable_append.description: Append to an existing variable's value.

automatic_at.description: Automatic variable: the file name of the target of the rule.

automatic_less.description: Automatic variable: the name of the first prerequisite.

automatic_caret.description: Automatic variable: the names of all prerequisites, with spaces between them.

automatic_star.description: Automatic variable: the stem with which an implicit rule matches.

shell.description: Execute a shell command and return its output as the function's value.

wildcard.description: Expands to a list of existing files matching the pattern.

patsubst.description: Pattern substitution - replace words matching pattern with replacement.

include.description: Include another Makefile. Make will suspend reading the current Makefile and process the included one.

ifeq.description: Conditional directive - evaluate the enclosed text only if the two arguments are equal.

pattern_rule.description: Pattern rules use % as a wildcard to define implicit rules for building files.

recipe_prefix.description: Recipe lines MUST start with a TAB character, not spaces. This is a common source of errors.

silent.description: Prefix a recipe line with @ to suppress echoing the command itself.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.
